basics.name: Nguyen Hong Phuc
basics.label: BSc ECE Student & AI Researcher
basics.summary: Electrical and Computer Engineering student at VinUniversity specializing in AI, machine learning, and signal processing. Research at ICCL Lab (VinUni & Trinity College Dublin) on semantic communication, aquaculture AI, and embedded systems. $3,500 Student Research Grant recipient.

work.name: Intelligence, Computing and Communication Lab (ICCL), VinUniversity & Trinity College Dublin
work.summary: Supervised by Assist. Prof. Nguyen Van Dinh.
highlights: Investigated Open RAN architecture, 5G networks, and Task-Oriented Semantic Communication.
Developed multi-modality AI models for smart aquaculture (computer vision, fish tracking, underwater size estimation, image segmentation).
Exploring Diffusion Models for enhanced data generation and signal processing applications.

work.name: FPT Software
highlights: Deployed quantized LLMs on Raspberry Pi to create a conversational conservation chatbot.
Engineered a full-stack solution (Frontend/Backend) for real-time shopper tracking and route optimization via BLE localization.

work.name: WorldQuant
highlights: Developed predictive mathematical models (Alphas) to forecast financial instrument price movements.
Utilized statistical analysis and historical data to optimize trading signals.

education.institution: Bac Ninh High School for the Gifted

awards.title: Best Poster Award — Open Innovation Conference 2025
Finalist — 2025 Quantum Global Industry Challenge (Connected DMV)
Silver Medal — The University Physics Competition 2024
2nd Prize — National Physics Olympiad
awards.awarder: Ministry of Education and Training, Vietnam

awards.title: 5th Place Worldwide — Thomas Jefferson Physics Olympiad

publications.name: AquaSC: Task-Oriented Semantic Communication for Efficient and Intelligent Aquaculture IoT Systems
publications.publisher: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
publications.summary: Under review. Task-oriented semantic communication framework for aquaculture IoT systems.

publications.name: AquaDeHi: Detached Hierarchical Multi-Task Learning with Learnable Prototypes for Fine-Grained Aquatic Species Recognition
publications.summary: Under review. Fine-grained aquatic species recognition via detached hierarchical multi-task learning.

publications.name: From Monitoring to Action: A Comprehensive Survey of Autonomous Decision-Making in Smart Aquaculture Systems
publications.summary: Under review. Comprehensive survey of autonomous decision-making for smart aquaculture.

publications.name: Energy-Aware Resource Allocation for Energy Harvesting Powered Wireless Sensor Nodes
publications.summary: Novel energy-aware resource allocation framework enabling asynchronous accumulate-then-transmit protocol for EH-powered WSNs.

publications.name: ChronoSC: Task-Oriented Semantic Communication via Temporal-to-Color Encoding
publications.summary: Accepted. Task-oriented semantic communication using temporal-to-color encoding.

publications.name: C-MambaPose: A Physics-Informed Complex Mamba Framework for Cross-Environment WiFi Human Pose Estimation
publications.summary: Physics-informed complex Mamba framework for WiFi-based human pose estimation.

projects.name: Smart Aquaculture IoT System
projects.summary: Multi-sensory IoT node with Multimodal AI, LLM RAG, and semantic communication. Funded by $3,500 Student Research Grant.

projects.name: Deep JSCC for Visible Light Communication
projects.summary: Hardware-in-the-loop Deep JSCC via analog light (R-2R DAC). 98% MNIST compression with differentiable channel approximation.

projects.name: Teensy 4.1 Flight Controller Firmware
projects.summary: Custom C++ firmware with 400Hz PID loop, Madgwick sensor fusion (MPU6050), and RC signal filtering for Quad-X drone.

projects.name: WinCart
projects.summary: AI-powered smart shopping assistant with ML-based positioning on Silicon Labs hardware.

projects.name: Neural Network From Scratch
projects.summary: CNN in C++ and feedforward NN in C for MNIST classification without external ML libraries.
